senses.definition: relating to protophytes, the earliest or simplest form of plant life that began to grow through photosynthesis
examples: Protophytic organisms laid the groundwork for later, more complex plants.
Some studies discuss protophytic stages in the evolution of land plants.

senses.definition: a chemical compound or ingredient used as an precursor in research, not widely used outside specialist contexts
examples: The lab synthesized protopin to study its reactions.
Protopin levels were measured as part of the experiment.

senses.definition: a natural alkaloid found in some plants, used in pharmacology and medicinal chemistry
examples: Protopine is studied for its potential medicinal properties.
The plant contains protopine among other alkaloids.

senses.definition: a planet in its early stage before it becomes a fully formed planet
examples: The protoplanet accreted material from the disk around the young star.
Astronomers study protoplanets to understand planet formation.

senses.definition: relating to or derived from a protoplanet; pertaining to the early stage of planet formation
examples: A protoplanetary disk surrounds many young stars.
Researchers study protoplanetary materials to learn about solar system origins.

senses.definition: plural of protoplanet; multiple early-stage planets
examples: The survey found several protoplanets in the disk.
Protoplanets eventually become full planets.

senses.definition: the living contents of a cell, excluding the nucleus, often used to describe the cell’s vital substance
examples: Protoplasm flows when the cell is active.
Scientists study the properties of protoplasm in living tissues.

senses.definition: alternative or historical spelling of protoplasm
examples: The term protoplasma appears in older texts.
Protoplasma was once used to describe the living substance of cells.

senses.definition: relating to protoplasm; composed of or resembling living cell substance
examples: Protoplasmal changes occur as cells respond to stimuli.
The protoplasmal layer protects the cell interior.

senses.definition: relating to protoplasm; of or resembling the living substance of cells
examples: Protoplasmatic movement indicates cellular activity.
Researchers examined protoplasmatic properties in the specimen.

senses.definition: plural of protoplasm; living contents inside cells
examples: All protoplasms within the tissues reacted to the stimulus.
Different cells contain varying protoplasms.

senses.definition: a living plant or bacterial cell whose wall has been removed
examples: Scientists study protoplasts to understand cell walls.
Protoplasts can be used in genetic experiments.

senses.definition: relating to protoplasts or to a form of early cell development
examples: Protoplastic changes were observed under the microscope.
The protoplastic stage precedes more complex cellular structures.

senses.definition: plural of protoplast; multiple wall-less plant or bacterial cells
examples: Researchers cultured several protoplasts for the experiment.
Protoplasts can fuse to form new cells.

senses.definition: a primary segment of a limb in some crustaceans or arthropods; the base of a limb
examples: The protopod forms the base of the leg in certain crustaceans.
Researchers described the protopod and its articulations.

senses.definition: relating to or forming a protopod
examples: Protopodial structures were identified in the specimen.
The protopodial region connects to other limb segments.

senses.definition: the distal segment of a crustacean limb that bears the tarsus
examples: The protopodite bears the terminal segments of the limb.
Anatomists noted the protopodite's articulation with the protopod.

senses.definition: relating to protopodites or protopoditic segments
examples: Protopoditic features were described in the fossil.
The study examined protopoditic joints.

senses.definition: plural of protopod; multiple limb base segments in crustaceans or arthropods
examples: The crustacean has two strong protopods in each limb.
Researchers compared the protopods across species.

senses.definition: relating to early or primitive poetry; of or resembling proto-poetic expression
examples: The manuscript shows protopoetic language from ancient times.
Scholars debate the protopoetic features of the text.

senses.definition: an archaic or fictional term; possibly a priest or spiritual leader in early contexts; not widely used in modern English
examples: In the legend, the protopope guided the villagers.
The term protopope appears in some old manuscripts.

senses.definition: a chemical compound that is a precursor to heme and chlorophyll, consisting of protoporphyrin plus iron or magnesium
examples: Protoporphyrin is a key component in biological pigments.
The spectroscopic analysis targeted protoporphyrin derivatives.

senses.definition: plural of protoporphyrin; multiple chemical compounds related to the protoporphyrin structure
examples: The assay detected several protoporphyrins in the sample.
Protoporphyrins play a role in various biological pigments.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or rare form; not a standard English term
examples: The manuscript used a proto‑pragmatic term that is not common in modern usage.
